title: Chapter 6 Quiz: Styling Deep Dive
description: Test your knowledge of Flexbox, custom fonts, platform‑specific styles, responsive design, and theming.

questions.question: What does `alignContent` control in a flex container?
options: Distribution of space along main axis
questions.explanation: `alignContent` aligns multiple lines when wrapping is enabled.

questions.question: What is the default value of `flexDirection` in React Native?
questions.explanation: Default is `column` (unlike web where it's `row`).

questions.question: How do you load a custom font in an Expo app?
options: Place the font in assets and use `fontFamily`
Add a @font-face rule in a CSS file
questions.explanation: Expo requires `expo-font` to load fonts; simply placing them isn't enough.

questions.question: Which prop adds a shadow on Android?
questions.explanation: Android uses `elevation` for shadows (and z‑order).

questions.question: What does `Platform.select({ ios: 10, android: 20 })` return on iOS?
questions.explanation: It returns the value for the current platform.

questions.question: Which hook gives you live window dimensions?
questions.explanation: `useWindowDimensions` updates on orientation changes.

questions.question: How can you maintain aspect ratio of a view?
questions.explanation: `aspectRatio` ensures the view maintains the given ratio.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a theme system?
options: To centralise design constants and support multiple themes
To automatically adapt to screen size
questions.explanation: Theming provides consistency and easy switching (e.g., dark mode).

questions.question: What does `flex: 1` mean in React Native?
questions.explanation: In React Native, `flex: 1` is equivalent to `flexGrow: 1, flexShrink: 1, flexBasis: 0`.

questions.question: Which statement about text style inheritance is true?
options: All styles (including layout) are inherited by nested Text
questions.explanation: Nested `<Text>` components inherit text styles, but not layout properties.

questions.question: The `Dimensions` API automatically updates on orientation change.
questions.explanation: `Dimensions.get` returns static values; you must listen to changes or use `useWindowDimensions`.

questions.question: You can use `Platform.OS` inside a `StyleSheet.create` call.
questions.explanation: Yes, you can use `Platform.OS` or `Platform.select` to define platform‑specific styles.

questions.question: Custom fonts must be loaded before the app renders to avoid a flash of default font.
questions.explanation: Using `useFonts` with a loading state prevents rendering until fonts are ready.

questions.question: Setting `position: 'absolute'` removes the item from the flexbox flow.
questions.explanation: Absolutely positioned items are taken out of the normal layout flow.

questions.question: The style property used to add a shadow on iOS is _____.
questions.explanation: iOS uses `shadowColor`, `shadowOffset`, `shadowOpacity`, `shadowRadius`.

questions.question: To truncate text after a certain number of lines, use the prop _____.
questions.explanation: `numberOfLines` limits the number of lines; combine with `ellipsizeMode`.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid `alignItems` value?
questions.explanation: `space-between` is for `justifyContent`, not `alignItems`.

questions.question: How do you create a grid of items with FlatList?
questions.explanation: `numColumns` divides the list into a grid.

questions.question: What does the `aspectRatio` style do?
options: Sets the ratio of width to height
Sets the ratio of the view's corner radius
Sets the ratio of padding to margin
Sets the ratio of font size
questions.explanation: `aspectRatio` maintains the proportion between width and height.

questions.question: Which pattern is recommended for theming?
options: React Context with a theme object
questions.explanation: Context provides a clean way to pass theme data deep into the component tree.
